creator: josh@quick.net
text:
Emil is a filter for converting Internet Messages. It supports
three basic formats: MIME, SUN Mailtool and RFC822. It can be
used with sendmail, as a loopback mailer, as a prefilter or
backend program with a mail client program, or as a plain
filter. Conversion can be configured by a configuration file,
emil.cf, using sender, recipient and recipient host as input
parameters or by command line arguments.

Emil is able to:

        convert the format, headers and structure, between
        messages of type MIME, Sun Mailtool and RFC822.

        convert the encoding of binary data between the types
        Base64, BinHex and Uuencode.

        convert the encoding of text to and from the MIME
        encoding Quoted-Printable.

        convert character set of text between the character sets
        made available by Keld J. Simonsens strncnv package. The
        strncnv package handles a large number of of character
        sets, as specified by RFC1345.

        do one-way conversions of 8bit text to the Swedish
        national variant of ISO-646.

        do one-way conversions of 8bit text to characters in
        US-ASCII of the closest resemblance.

        convert to and from RFC1522 format headers.

Fix: 

ftp://ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/incoming/emil-port.tar.gz

MD5 (emil-port.tar.gz) = e91c283ec52b51d11d978cb88baae326
